conversationalSummary.text: Tomorrow is probably one to skip. Cerro at After work (17:30 start) looks like the least-bad option, but not really something worth chasing. The wind looks like the main issue and could easily make it messy. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam first, but unless it looks surprisingly clean, I'd save the drive.

best.windFit: N 30 km/h · cross-onshore · too windy, strong
best.bestWind: ideal SSW, usable S/SW/SSE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Cerro looks like a solid window for Worth checking, Low confidence: 1.2-1.5 m, 272 kJ, 10 s period, consistent 74/100, and wind is the main risk.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 10-13s at mid tide works well on exposed beaches.  Watch: wind is over 30 km/h; wind fit is poor or too strong; gusts over 45 km/h add chop risk.
best.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for after work (17:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind fit is poor or too strong
gusts over 45 km/h add chop risk
